name: New York Common Pantry
hours: Check website before visiting — hours vary by program
eligibility: Open to all NYC residents. No ID required for emergency food.
notes: One of NYC's largest food pantries. Operates a client-choice model in which guests shop like in a grocery store, selecting foods that fit their family's needs and culture.

name: Coalition for the Homeless
hours: Grand Central Food Program: nightly 8:30 pm – 10:00 pm (year-round)
eligibility: No requirements — open to anyone who is hungry or unhoused.
notes: The Grand Central Food Program has served meals every night without exception for over 40 years. No registration or ID required. Meals are distributed near Grand Central Terminal.

name: City Harvest
hours: Mobile markets: check cityharvest.org/get-help/find-food for current locations and times
eligibility: No requirements at mobile markets. Just show up.
notes: City Harvest rescues millions of pounds of surplus food each year from restaurants, farms, and grocery stores, then distributes through a network of over 600 community food programs across NYC.

name: Lenox Hill Neighborhood House
eligibility: Open to Upper East Side and East Harlem residents. Photo ID and proof of address are helpful but not required for first visit.
notes: A century-old settlement house offering integrated social services alongside food support. Known for its warmth and community-centered approach.

name: The Bowery Mission
hours: Meals served daily — check bowery.org for current service times
eligibility: No requirements for meals. Walk in anytime meals are being served. Shelter and recovery programs have an intake process.
notes: Serving New York's most vulnerable since 1879. Walk-in meals are available daily with no registration required. A place where everyone is welcomed with dignity.

name: Food Bank For New York City
hours: Check foodbanknyc.org/get-help/find-food for community partner hours near you
eligibility: No requirements at food distribution sites. SNAP assistance available for eligible households.
notes: The Food Bank operates NYC's largest hunger-relief network, supplying over 800 community food programs citywide. Their online Find Food tool helps you locate the nearest open pantry or kitchen.

name: West Side Campaign Against Hunger
hours: Check website before visiting — appointment system may be in use
eligibility: Open to anyone on Manhattan's west side. Some appointments required — call or check website.
notes: Uses a client-choice model: guests shop like in a grocery store, selecting items that fit their family's dietary needs and cultural preferences. A dignified, respectful approach to food assistance.

name: Holy Apostles Soup Kitchen
hours: Monday – Friday, lunch service — check website for current times
eligibility: No requirements. Open to all who are hungry.
notes: One of NYC's largest soup kitchens, serving up to 1,100 hot meals per day. Run almost entirely by volunteers inside an Episcopal church. A community gathering place as much as a meal program.

name: St. John's Bread & Life
eligibility: Open to anyone in need. No documentation required for meals.
notes: Brooklyn's largest soup kitchen and food pantry, serving the Bushwick and Bed-Stuy communities. A stable, trusted presence in a neighborhood that needs it.

name: Part of the Solution (POTS)
eligibility: Open to all Bronx residents. Pantry may have neighborhood preference.
notes: A beloved Bronx institution in the Fordham Heights community offering food alongside wraparound services — case management, legal help, and connections to housing and healthcare.

name: Xavier Mission
hours: Saturday and Sunday meals — check website for current times
notes: A welcoming community that has served weekend meals since 1993. Known for its warmth and dignity-centered approach — guests are neighbors, not numbers.

name: CHiPS Soup Kitchen & Women's Shelter
eligibility: Soup kitchen open to all. Shelter is for women experiencing homelessness.
notes: One of Brooklyn's longest-running soup kitchens and an emergency women's shelter. CHiPS has been a lifeline for Brooklyn families since 1971.

name: Rethink Food
hours: Check rethinkfood.org for current distribution sites and hours
notes: Founded by a Michelin-starred chef, Rethink Food partners with restaurants and food businesses to rescue surplus food and distribute nutritious, restaurant-quality meals to communities across NYC.

name: God's Love We Deliver
address: 166 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10013
hours: Deliveries on weekdays — call to apply for service
eligibility: For people living with serious illness (HIV/AIDS, cancer, heart disease, and others). Free of charge. Call to apply — a brief intake process confirms eligibility.
notes: Provides medically tailored meals, developed by registered dietitians, to people who are too ill to shop or cook. Serving New York City since 1985. Completely free, no income requirement.

name: Encore Community Services
eligibility: Primarily for adults 60+ in Manhattan. Call to learn about programs and eligibility.
notes: Dedicated to older New Yorkers, Encore provides hot meals in a warm community setting and delivers to homebound seniors. Meals are accompanied by social connection — a crucial part of the program.

name: Community Kitchen of West Harlem
eligibility: Open to all West Harlem and surrounding community residents.
notes: Serves Harlem with hot meals and fresh groceries while running culinary workforce training programs — giving community members both food and a path toward employment in the food industry.

name: NYC Rescue Mission
hours: Check website for current meal service hours
eligibility: No requirements for meals. Shelter and recovery programs have an intake process.
notes: One of New York's oldest missions, operating since 1872. Provides meals, shelter, and long-term recovery support for the city's most vulnerable residents.
